## LibraryData.ILibraryLoadParams interface

Fine-grained parameters for controlling which collections from a library to load.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export interface ILibraryLoadParams 
```

Property

</td><td>

`readonly`


</td><td>

ReadonlyArray&lt;[FilterPattern](./ts-chocolate.librarydata.filterpattern.md)<!-- -->&gt;


</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Patterns to exclude. Collection names matching any pattern are excluded (takes precedence over included). Strings are matched exactly, RegExp patterns use `.test()`<!-- -->.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[included?](./ts-chocolate.librarydata.ilibraryloadparams.included.md)


</td><td>

`readonly`


</td><td>

ReadonlyArray&lt;[FilterPattern](./ts-chocolate.librarydata.filterpattern.md)<!-- -->&gt;


</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Patterns to include. If specified, only collection names matching at least one pattern are included. Strings are matched exactly, RegExp patterns use `.test()`<!-- -->.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[recurseWithDelimiter?](./ts-chocolate.librarydata.ilibraryloadparams.recursewithdelimiter.md)


</td><td>

`readonly`


</td><td>

string


</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Whether to recurse into subdirectories and use a delimiter to form composite collection names.
